stem: A decision-maker must choose among 4 project methodology options. The probability of favorable team experience and project size is uncertain, estimated between 38% and 50%. The payoffs (in $K) for each option are: Agile: $81K in good team experience and project size, $17K in bad team experience and project size. Waterfall: $242K in good team experience and project size, $64K in bad team experience and project size. Hybrid: $199K in good team experience and project size, $13K in bad team experience and project size. Kanban: $217K in good team experience and project size, $80K in bad team experience and project size. Which option maximizes expected value? Does the optimal choice change across the range of probability estimates? What is the expected value of the best option?

ground_truth.answer: At P(good)=0.44: best option is Waterfall with EV=$142.3K. The optimal choice switches: Kanban at P(good)=0.38, Waterfall at P(good)=0.5. EV range of best option: [132.1, 153.0].

solution_steps: Compute EV for each option: EV = P(good)×payoff_good + P(bad)×payoff_bad.
The optimal choice switches: Kanban at P(good)=0.38, Waterfall at P(good)=0.5.
common_failure_modes: Evaluating options only at the midpoint probability without sensitivity analysis
Not identifying the breakeven probability where the optimal choice switches
Ignoring downside risk and focusing only on expected value
Not performing minimax regret analysis to find the most robust choice
